diagrams.title: Binary Tree Traversal Orders
diagrams.description: A binary tree with root 10, left child 5 (with left=2, right=7), right child 15 (with left=12, right=20) showing all four traversal orders
diagrams.purpose: Visualize and compare the output of different tree traversal algorithms on the same tree structure

diagrams.title: Level-Order Traversal Using Queue
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization showing how queue changes during level-order traversal of a sample tree
elements: Step 1: Dequeue Root, visit, Enqueue Left, Right
Step 2: Dequeue Left, visit, Enqueue its children
Step 3: Dequeue Right, visit, Enqueue its children
diagrams.purpose: Understand the mechanics of BFS/level-order traversal and the FIFO behavior of queue

diagrams.title: Deep Copy vs Shallow Copy
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing original tree and two types of copies with memory addresses
elements: Shallow copy: new tree pointing to same addresses 1001, 1002, 1003
Deep copy: new tree at addresses 2001, 2002, 2003 (different memory)
Arrow showing modification in deep copy doesn't affect original
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the difference between shallow and deep copying, and why deep copy creates independent data structures
